The right side depicts a stack, labeled 'stack,' which is a last-in, first-out (LIFO) data structure.  The expression is evaluated from left to right, with operands and operators being pushed onto the stack according to operator precedence and parentheses. The stack's role is to temporarily store operands and operators until they are needed for calculation, enabling the correct order of operations to be maintained.
